title: Rebuild as a single closed_loop.py module
body: Replace the current multi-stage pipeline (extract_*.py → calibrate_weights.py → align_parallel.py → gap_analysis.py → validate_against_comments.py → write_reports.py) with a single module scripts/closed_loop.py exposing State and step(state) → state'. Iteration to fixed point. Existing scripts/ files remain as prior-run artifacts for comparison but are not called by the new loop.

title: Atoms start with τ = σ (symmetric at grade 1)
body: For atomic observations derived from verbatim parse-tree kind strings, τ_K(thing) = σ_K(thing) = 'kind K appears in thing'. No asymmetric channel is imposed at the atomic level. Asymmetry emerges ONLY via residue-driven wedge articulation at higher grades, where K_i ∧ K_j takes τ from one parent and σ from the other by the exterior-algebra combinator.

title: Things = named declarations, not every parse-tree node
body: For this first-pass rebuild, 'things' (the domain of alignment) are top-level named declarations across all sources: pandoc Div.{definition,theorem,...}, agda module/function/record/data/postulate, python FunctionDef/ClassDef/Module/Assign. Their atomic observations are every kind string appearing in their subtree. Using every parse-tree node would scale O(n²) in pair comparison to hundreds-of-thousands squared. Can be relaxed later if residue analysis suggests finer granularity matters.

title: Initial atoms = verbatim kind strings from subtrees
body: The initial K pool is the set of kind strings observed anywhere in any thing's subtree. No source prefix, no lowercasing, no canonicalization. 'Module' and 'module' are different K's; 'FunctionDef' and 'function' are different K's. Any correspondence between them must emerge via wedge discovery across co-firing residue patterns.

title: No commit/tier/triple output
body: The pipeline does not output a filtered set of committed triples. It outputs the full Belnap state: every K in the pool with provenance and firing distribution; every thing-pair's four-cell profile under every K-pair; a highlight of which (pair, K-pair) combinations are Boolean-earned; a trajectory log of κ-articulations. Reports are pretty-printed views of that state.

title: Wedge K fires iff both parents fire (AND semantics for profile inclusion)
body: When a wedge K_i ∧ K_j is articulated, its firing predicate is K_i(thing) AND K_j(thing). This is the indicator function for the grade-2 element being in the thing's profile. The exterior algebra combinator for τ/σ of the wedge (τ_A·σ_B + σ_A·τ_B in GF(2)) is a separate question handled when grade-2 chirality semantics are switched on — initially the wedge's τ = σ = the AND of parents.

title: Termination = no productive wedge articulation
body: step() returns a state; iteration compares state.pool to previous state.pool. When no new wedges are articulated AND weights have stabilized below an epsilon, loop terminates. No iteration counter, no max steps. Termination is a property of the state evolution, not an external schedule.

title: Citation oracle calibrates weights, does not gate commits
body: The authorial citation data (agda/python docstrings referencing paper definitions) is used only to tune per-K weights — specifically, weights adjust in the direction that increases Belnap evidence on oracle pairs. The oracle never participates in the commit/reject decision (because there is no such decision). Validation against the oracle is a post-hoc query of the final state, not a pipeline stage.

title: Wedge combinator is the general exterior-algebra form
body: For a wedge K = A ∧ B, the channel combinators are: τ_K(X) = τ_A(X)·σ_B(X) + σ_A(X)·τ_B(X) (GF(2)), σ_K(X) = τ_A(X)·τ_B(X) + σ_A(X)·σ_B(X), κ_K(X) = τ_K(X) + σ_K(X). Under τ = σ atoms this degenerates (τ_K = 0, σ_K = τ_A·τ_B); under asymmetric parents it generalizes. Do not simplify at grade 2 for the τ = σ case; implement the general form so higher-grade states with τ ≠ σ are supported uniformly.

title: Articulation scorer is pluggable; multiple scorers can run simultaneously
body: Define an articulation protocol that accepts a pluggable scorer of type (state, candidate_wedge) → float. Candidate scorers include: four-cell entropy reduction, oracle-pair cell-population increase, mutual information beyond independence, weighted residue decrease. Step() can run multiple scorers in parallel and articulate wedges from the union of their proposals. The scorer is NOT fixed by framework; initial implementation provides a small registry of scorers with a default composition.

title: Weight-adjustment objective is pluggable
body: Weights on K's tune toward an objective scalar that is pluggable, not fixed. Candidate objectives: Shannon entropy of the four-cell distribution across things; oracle-pair expected Belnap cell agreement; total Boolean-earning witness count on oracle pairs. Multiple objectives can be evaluated per iteration; their gradients can be combined as weighted sums. No single objective is privileged by the framework.

title: Reports dump the full state as queryable JSONL; views are separate
body: The closed-loop module outputs the full state (pool, per-thing τ/σ/κ profiles, per-K-pair per-thing-pair Belnap cell distributions, trajectory of articulations) as bulk JSONL. Presentation views (top-N by information measure, per-K-pair histograms, thing-pair profile clusterings) are separate query scripts that walk the JSONL. This keeps the framework output maximally free of interpretation — anyone can write their own view without the framework having prejudged a canonical report format.

title: Smoke-test corpus subset is a parameter, not a fixed choice
body: The closed-loop module accepts a corpus-subset selector (glob over source paths, id filter, random sample size). Smoke tests run at multiple parameterized scales. No single 'canonical smoke test' is prescribed; the smallest instructive instance at any time is the one the operator selects.

title: State data structure is designed for restrict/merge from the start
body: State exposes three core operations beyond step(): (a) restrict(things=..., k_pool=...) → sub-State over the requested slice; (b) merge(other) → State combining both; (c) hash/identity for fixed-point detection. All three must be pure. Merge is associative and commutative. restrict o step == step o restrict for partition-compatible scorers (those that don't require cross-partition joint distributions). Scorers that need cross-partition info are flagged and skipped by parallel shards; reconciliation step() in the joining session applies them.

title: JSONL on disk is the merge protocol; append-only, no explicit join step
body: State is persisted as append-only JSONL files (pool.jsonl, belnap.jsonl, trajectory.jsonl, weights.jsonl). Each work cell appends its output; no cell rewrites existing lines. Loading is a concatenation of all lines; duplicate keys resolved by semantic merge (sum Belnap counts; union K-pool by key; latest weight wins or averaged). Running a new session = loading the current JSONL, running step() on some partition, appending the diff. Fixed point = no further appends across a full corpus pass.

title: K implemented as abstract class with Atom/Wedge subclasses (recursive inductive type)
body: In Python: K is an abstract base class; Atom(key: str) and Wedge(a: K, b: K) are frozen dataclass subclasses.  This mirrors the inductive type K = Atom(str) | Wedge(K, K).  Self-similarity is structural: atoms and wedges are both K's, and every operation on K recurses over the structure.  Equality is intensional by default (dataclass __eq__ on constructor arguments).  Extensional equality via S3 or wedge-commutativity is provided by separate normalize() functions returning canonical representatives.

title: TauSigma type enforces κ = τ ⊕ σ at the type level
body: TauSigma is a frozen dataclass with tau: int and sigma: int as bitmasks; kappa is a derived property computed as tau ^ sigma.  No code path stores kappa independently.  The F₂² constraint is thus maintained by construction — an invariant of the type, not a discipline of the callers.  S3 action is a method act(g: S3) → TauSigma that permutes which two of (τ, σ, κ) are 'primary' while preserving the underlying F₂² vector.
